en_body:
I wish, today, to make for you
a poem about politics
In the sweet language I learned to speak from Mother and Father
The language in which long ago I cried and fretted with Grandmother
The language in which I made poems when I became a mother


I thought the poem would be very long
Yet short enough to love the poem too much
And the poem ends
When I say Love-You.
note: Politics is returned to the inherited Vietnamese language of family, motherhood, and intimate Love.
